evals.prompt: Create an advanced output set with a scalar 'auc' from AUC_drug and an objective 'obj_auc' targeting auc between 8 and 12 (wide range 5-15), weight 1.0.
evals.expected_output: Adds a scalar component with formula AUC_drug, then an objective targeting it with the given narrow/wide range bounds and weight, via client.create_advanced_output_set or scoring_design.components.add_scalar/add_objective, and mentions that expressions are validated automatically and raise ValidationError on failure regardless of show_validation.

evals.prompt: Add a constraint restricting eligible patients to age >= 18 to my existing advanced output set sc-abc123 and show me if there are any diagnostic errors.
evals.expected_output: Calls scoring_design.components.add_constraint('adults', constraint='age >= 18'), then reads scoring_design.diagnostics.errors() (or .explain()) to report issues, without inventing model output ids.

evals.prompt: I have model cm-abc123 and want a simple output set with the Drug output's AUC over the full simulation plus its end value.
evals.expected_output: Uses model.time_dependent_ids() only if ids are unspecified, and otherwise builds two measure dicts for Drug: one with function AcrossTime/Auc/FromStartUntilEnd and one with PointAtTime TEnd, via client.create_simple_output_set or model.create_simple_output_set. Does not invent an output id not present on the model.

evals.prompt: Now attach that simple output set to a trial on the same model and run it.
evals.expected_output: Declines to perform trial creation/run in this skill and routes the user to jk-trial, e.g. model.create_trial(simple_output_set=..., ...) then trial.run(), instead of implementing trial creation here.
